subject: Accounting
title: Capital Gains Tax
description: A quick win: understand Capital Gains Tax.

hook.text: You buy stock for $1,000 and sell it for $5,000. The $4,000 profit gets taxed—but how much?

buildup.text: Capital gains tax applies to profits from selling assets like stocks, real estate, or businesses.

discovery.text: Hold the asset over a year and the tax rate drops. Short-term gains are taxed like regular income.

twist.text: Billionaires often pay lower tax rates than employees because most of their wealth is in long-term gains.

climax.text: The debate over capital gains rates sits at the heart of tax fairness discussions worldwide.

punchline.text: Patience pays—literally. Hold longer, pay less tax.

quiz.question: Why are long-term capital gains taxed at lower rates?
